finding: PAPER ledger values can be known while dashboard freshness and continuous runtime proof remain stale or missing.

defect_classification.defect: Operators could read stale PAPER values as either fully current or completely unverified.
defect_classification.operational_risk: Portfolio review becomes confusing and may overtrust an old simulated snapshot.
defect_classification.closure: Display value truth, snapshot freshness, runtime continuity, and writer lifecycle as separate fields.

defect_classification.defect: Existing audited snapshot output and inactive continuous writer status appeared contradictory.
defect_classification.operational_risk: Operator may believe the current-evidence writer is live when only an audited snapshot exists.

defect_classification.defect: A strict 300 second freshness limit made old PAPER snapshots hard to interpret.
defect_classification.operational_risk: The dashboard looked broken even when last verified simulated values were available.
defect_classification.closure: Keep the 300 second safety threshold but label stale values as last verified, not runtime proof.

acceptance_conditions: fresh PAPER ledger values must use PAPER_LEDGER_CURRENT_VALUES_VERIFIED
stale PAPER ledger values must use PAPER_LEDGER_LAST_VERIFIED_VALUES_STALE
portfolio without snapshot provenance must use CURRENT_VALUES_UNVERIFIED_CONFIGURED_BASELINE_ONLY
audited current evidence snapshot display must disclose continuous writer blocked
runtime continuity cannot be inferred from a snapshot
